feedback.claim: The hashing section does not specify which hash algorithm the system uses.
feedback.evidence: The entire section reads 'The system hashes the draft.' No algorithm such as SHA-256, SHA-512, or any other is named.
feedback.invariant_violated: A hashing specification must name the hash algorithm to be reproducible across implementations.
feedback.recommended_change: Add a sentence naming the algorithm, e.g. 'The system hashes the draft using SHA-256.'
feedback.severity_rationale: Without a named algorithm no two implementations can produce or verify matching digests, rendering the hashing specification non-functional.

feedback.claim: The section does not define what bytes or representation of 'the draft' are fed into the hash function.
feedback.evidence: 'The system hashes the draft' is silent on encoding, normalization, field inclusion, and byte ordering.
feedback.invariant_violated: A hashing specification must define the exact byte sequence used as input to the hash function.
feedback.recommended_change: Specify the canonical input form, e.g. 'The system hashes the UTF-8 encoded bytes of the draft with line endings normalized to LF and no trailing whitespace.'
feedback.severity_rationale: Without a defined input representation two systems hashing semantically identical drafts may produce different digests, making verification impossible.

feedback.claim: The spec permits non-deterministic hashing because it leaves algorithm, encoding, and normalization undefined.
feedback.evidence: The single sentence 'The system hashes the draft' constrains none of the parameters that must be fixed for deterministic output.
feedback.invariant_violated: Hashing must be fully deterministic: identical logical inputs must always produce identical digests across all compliant implementations.
feedback.recommended_change: Constrain the section to mandate a specific algorithm and a canonical input form so that any two compliant implementations produce identical digests for the same draft content.
feedback.severity_rationale: A hash used for verification that can vary across implementations provides no verification guarantee and blocks all downstream logic that depends on it.

feedback.claim: The smoke spec contains only a hashing subsection and no other content, leaving purpose, scope, actors, and behavioral rules entirely unspecified.
feedback.evidence: The document body consists solely of the heading '## Hashing' and one sentence. No goals, actors, invariants, or non-hashing behavioral rules appear anywhere in the document.
feedback.invariant_violated: A specification must define the scope, purpose, and governing rules of the system it describes.
feedback.recommended_change: Add a top-level section describing the purpose of the smoke spec, what system behavior it governs, and any invariants or rules beyond hashing.
feedback.severity_rationale: A spec with no defined scope or purpose cannot guide implementation or review; the omission is major but does not itself invalidate the hashing subsection's content.
